content: Multiple existing open-source SAM2 napari plugins provide click-to-segment + click-correction UX directly applicable as reference implementations: napari-sam (MIC-DKFZ, SAM1), napari-SAMV2 and napari-sam2long (point clicks: positive/negative labels via click or keyboard shortcut, e.g. 'a' for add / 'n' for negative), napari-vos-sam2 (middle-click positive, Ctrl+middle-click negative), FrancisCrickInstitute napari_sam2 (keybinds for speed, proper state reset when adding/removing prompts), and Interactive Medical-SAM2 GUI / SAMannot (open-source local desktop apps built on napari with point/box prompts and brush/eraser correction tools). These establish the standard UX vocabulary: left-click=positive point, modifier-click=negative point, brush/eraser for manual mask cleanup, keyboard shortcuts for speed.
